title: Mildly Smoked Ethiopian Lamb Skewers
description: A romantic Ethiopian dish featuring sesame seeds and a hint of smokiness, perfect for a special occasion. This dish is inspired by traditional Ethiopian cuisine and is friendly to Buddhist temple dietary restrictions.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, salt, and black pepper.
Add the lamb cubes to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated lamb cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Sprinkle the sesame seeds and dum spice mix over the lamb skewers.
Place the skewers on the robata grill and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until the lamb is cooked to an internal temperature of 145 degrees Fahrenheit.
Flip the skewers halfway through cooking to ensure even cooking.
Once cooked, remove the skewers from the grill and let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
